campaign.name: Thirty Days of Fieldwork with Blue
campaign.primary_audience: U.S. adults ages 18–25 who already use online media for learning, psychology, self-reflection, or creator-led education
campaign.core_definition: Mental Wealth Academy is a social-first gameworld for learning, research, and rewards.
campaign.blue_radio_position: Blue Radio is the Academy's ambient platform field guide: a synchronized, character-led orientation broadcast with ten prerecorded chapters in the current episode.
campaign.primary_goal: Move qualified viewers into one first meaningful action: open a guide, start a mission, write a field note, inspect a quest, or tune in to Blue Radio.
campaign.operating_rule: Every post must teach one useful idea, demonstrate one real product action, document one learning, or ask one precise research question. Produce one master asset per day; cross-post only where the same asset fits without a second shoot or new research pass.

channel_roles.YouTube: Anchor channel for searchable explanations, product walkthroughs, Shorts, replays, and future broadcast archives.
channel_roles.TikTok and Instagram Reels: Discovery through 20–45 second concept-first clips with captions and one next action.
channel_roles.Instagram carousel: Saveable research cards, product maps, and source limitations.
channel_roles.Reddit and aligned communities: Native, useful discussions and focused feedback requests after learning each community's rules.
channel_roles.Discord: Existing-member prompts, field checks, and consented artifact discussion.
channel_roles.Blue Radio: In-product orientation, recurring host presence, and source material for clips; the current episode is prerecorded and loops through the day and night.
channel_roles.Founder account: Transparent build notes, corrections, and user-learning updates.

measurement.primary_value_event: First meaningful action completed within 24 hours of a content click
measurement.weekly_review: Qualified visits, action completion, seven-day return, saves, substantive replies, Blue Radio tune-ins, and content-to-product conversion
measurement.reach_rule: Impressions diagnose distribution; they do not prove learning value.
measurement.test_rule: Alternate midday and evening Eastern Time windows for the first two weeks, then keep the better window by qualified action rate. No research source establishes a universal best posting time.

posts.objective: Name the month and collect the audience's real questions.
posts.hook: What is one question you wish school had helped you investigate?
posts.caption: For the next 30 days, Mental Wealth Academy is running a field lab in public. One question. One useful idea. One action inside the Academy. Bring a question about psychology, media, attention, habits, or the systems shaping your choices.
posts.visual_direction: Open on a blank field-note page, cut to Blue Radio, the guide map, and one mission card. End on the campaign title and date range.
posts.cta: Reply with one question. We will select questions that can be answered with a credible source and one useful Academy action this month.
posts.guardrail: Before posting, set a written weekly selection limit. Do not promise that every question will be answered.
posts.repurpose: Pin as the YouTube channel trailer for the month; post the question alone in Discord and one relevant community.

posts.objective: Define the brand idea through a concrete reflective practice.
posts.hook: Make one action observable.
posts.caption: Choose one action from today: a page of notes, a question you researched, or a decision you recorded. Write what happened and what changed. A field note turns reflection into an observation you can revisit.
posts.visual_direction: One concrete action per card. Final card shows the field-note screen with observation, interpretation, and meaning.
posts.cta: Write one field note about an action you took today.
posts.guardrail: Present this as a reflection practice, not a clinical outcome or financial measure.
posts.repurpose: Use the first card as a Story prompt and the full carousel as a YouTube Community post.

posts.objective: Introduce Blue Radio with an accurate, memorable product demonstration.
posts.hook: Five minutes. Ten chapters. One map of the Academy.
posts.caption: Blue Radio is a synchronized field guide inside the Academy. The current prerecorded episode runs 4 minutes and 46 seconds across ten chapters about the course, field notes, quests, credits, the guide library, Blue, and community. Tune in anywhere in the loop, then open one feature she names.
posts.visual_direction: Show the Live tab, chapter titles changing, the audio-reactive avatar, and the 4:46 runtime in a small factual label.
posts.cta: Tune in from the Live tab and choose one feature to open.
posts.guardrail: Label the current episode prerecorded. Avoid live tutor, interactive stream, personalized educator, and 24 hours of unique programming.
posts.repurpose: Publish a 2–5 minute YouTube walkthrough showing every chapter title.
posts.kpi: Blue Radio tune-ins and post-listen feature opens

posts.objective: Show how curiosity becomes a path through the guide map.
posts.hook: Your curiosity sets the syllabus.
posts.caption: Start with one published guide. Follow its published prerequisites back to the foundations. When you complete every direct published prerequisite for another guide, the map marks that guide ready. Your curiosity still chooses the route.
posts.visual_direction: Record one real route through the public guide map. Show prerequisite labels and the next unlocked guide without exposing member data.
posts.cta: Open one published guide from the map.
posts.guardrail: Say 'each published topic has one canonical guide.' Do not claim every topic is verified or reviewed by PhD students.
posts.repurpose: Cut the path reveal into a 25-second vertical clip and a three-card map carousel.

posts.objective: Teach one media-literacy principle with a primary source.
posts.hook: A countdown means something only when a real condition ends.
posts.caption: False low-stock messages, baseless demand claims, and resetting countdowns are pressure patterns. A legitimate deadline names the event, capacity, or review window that actually closes. Inspect the next urgent screen you see: what changes when the clock reaches zero?
posts.visual_direction: Show three generic interface patterns drawn from the FTC taxonomy. Use diagrams rather than copying a brand's live ad.
posts.cta: Inspect one urgent message today and write down what changes when its clock reaches zero.
posts.proof_or_source: FTC, Bringing Dark Patterns to Light (2022), pp. 20–22; research/content-calendar/source-files/ftc-bringing-dark-patterns-to-light-2022.pdf
posts.guardrail: Describe U.S. FTC staff guidance accurately. Avoid giving legal advice or accusing a named company without case-specific evidence.
posts.repurpose: Post the inspection question natively in one media-literacy community after reviewing its rules.

posts.objective: Make field notes tangible through a founder-owned example.
posts.hook: A field note begins where the polished answer stops.
posts.caption: Here is one founder field note from this week's research. Continuous audio can create a recurring station inside the Academy. Searchable clips may give new viewers a clearer entry point, so the next test turns one broadcast chapter into a clip with a direct route to the feature it explains.
posts.visual_direction: Show James's own note, then the clip storyboard it produced. Remove private details before capture.
posts.cta: Write one observation, one interpretation, and one next action.
posts.guardrail: Hold until the dated founder field note and the clip test it proposes are stored. Use only James's own writing unless another participant gives explicit permission.
posts.repurpose: Publish the note as a text post on the founder account.

posts.objective: Turn fact-checking into visible trust.
posts.hook: We cut a claim because the evidence did not support it.
posts.caption: The landing page described U.S. literacy as a grade level. The national source measures proficiency levels instead. The corrected fact is narrower: in 2017, 19% of assessed U.S. adults ages 16–65 performed at Level 1 or below in English literacy. A smaller claim with a named measure is more useful.
posts.visual_direction: Show the crossed-out sentence, the NCES table, and the corrected source card with population and year.
posts.cta: Open the source and check the measure before sharing the statistic.
posts.guardrail: Do not imply the 19% figure is a sixth-grade reading level or a 2026 estimate.
posts.repurpose: Add the correction to a permanent Research Receipts playlist.

posts.objective: Demonstrate the synchronized-station mechanic.
posts.hook: Open Blue Radio on two devices. The same chapter is already playing.
posts.caption: The program follows the clock. Listeners who tune in at roughly the same moment arrive at the same point in the loop. It is a small shared-time ritual inside an otherwise self-paced Academy.
posts.visual_direction: Place two devices side by side with visible chapter titles and clocks. Record in one take.
posts.cta: Tune in and post the chapter title you found.
posts.guardrail: Say roughly the same moment. Network and playback conditions can create small differences.
posts.repurpose: Turn the replies into a same-chapter collage without displaying private account information.

posts.objective: Explain why the campaign uses creator-led video without flattening young adults into a stereotype.
posts.hook: Reach maps distribution. Completed actions show learning value.
posts.caption: Pew found that 95% of U.S. adults ages 18–29 use YouTube, 80% use Instagram, and 63% use TikTok. These 2025 figures measure self-reported platform use. We will compare the channels by meaningful actions completed inside the Academy.
posts.visual_direction: Chart the three percentages with a visible label: U.S. adults 18–29, self-reported ever use, 2025.
posts.cta: Choose where you want the next full explanation: YouTube, Instagram, or TikTok.
posts.proof_or_source: Pew Research Center, Social Media Fact Sheet and Americans' Social Media Use 2025
posts.guardrail: Do not relabel ages 18–29 as ages 18–25. Include the survey year and 'ever use' definition.
posts.repurpose: Use the result to choose where the Day 13 explainer premieres.
posts.kpi: Qualified votes and subsequent action rate by channel

posts.objective: Teach a complete field-note action.
posts.format: Screen tutorial with a real prompt
posts.hook: Give the blank page three handles: fact, feeling, meaning.
posts.caption: Write what happened without interpretation. Name the feeling with the most precise word you have. End with what you made the event mean. The three lines separate observation from interpretation so you can inspect the difference.
posts.visual_direction: Type a safe example into the actual field-note surface. Keep the entry fictional and label it as a demonstration.
posts.cta: Open Field Notes and write three lines.
posts.guardrail: Describe a reflection practice, not treatment. Add care-routing language when the topic involves trauma or crisis.
posts.repurpose: Publish the three-line template as a saveable Story and Discord prompt.

posts.objective: Show the implemented progress-based guide recommendation.
posts.hook: Finish one guide. Blue can show what the completion unlocked.
posts.caption: The guide map records completed guides and exposes the next published frontier. Blue can show up to three next unlocked guides from that progress. The reason stays visible, so the suggestion has an inspectable path.
posts.visual_direction: Use a test account. Show the completed guide, the unlocked frontier, and the dashboard recommendation card with its completion-derived reason. Keep personal notes off screen.
posts.cta: Complete one published guide and inspect your next unlocked options.
posts.guardrail: Do not claim Blue reads field-note meaning to generate a personalized curriculum. The current recommendation uses recorded guide completions.
posts.repurpose: Create a three-card carousel: completed, unlocked, why.

posts.objective: Show a contribution improving a guide.
posts.hook: One good question can improve the path for the next learner.
posts.caption: A reader found a step that assumed too much prior knowledge. The guide now defines the missing term and adds a concrete example. The contribution matters because someone else can follow the path with less guesswork.
posts.visual_direction: Show a real before-and-after diff with the contributor's permission, or use the founder's own revision if no permission exists.
posts.cta: Open a guide and submit one precise question about a confusing step.
posts.proof_or_source: A real dated guide revision and consent record required before publication
posts.guardrail: Hold until the dated revision, attribution preference, and permission are documented. If using a founder-owned revision, rewrite the caption in first person and identify it as founder work.
posts.repurpose: Add the revision to a monthly changelog.
posts.kpi: Useful guide questions and approved revisions

posts.objective: Explain the layered role of video and text.
posts.hook: Use video for the map. Keep text for the exact turn.
posts.caption: A small experiment with 42 software-engineering students found video faster to apply and text faster to consume; participants preferred video for new material and text for lookup. The sample is narrow. The design lesson is worth testing: Blue Radio for orientation, transcripts and guides for precise reference.
posts.visual_direction: Split screen between a Blue Radio chapter and the exact guidebook section it points toward.
posts.cta: Tell us which moment you would rather watch and which one you would rather scan.
posts.guardrail: State the sample size and software-learning context. Present the layered interface as a test, not a universal rule.
posts.repurpose: Cut the central comparison into a 30-second Short and an Instagram poll.
posts.kpi: Watch time, saves, and format-preference responses

posts.objective: Demonstrate one complete mission route.
posts.hook: Start with one mission.
posts.caption: Open one current quest. Read the task, eligibility rules, reward conditions, and review requirements. Do the work and submit the evidence the quest asks for. One complete route teaches more than a feature list.
posts.visual_direction: Use a current low-risk mission or a clearly labeled demo mission. Show the criteria before the submission action.
posts.cta: Choose one open mission with a completion state you understand.
posts.guardrail: Hold until the demonstrated production mission is confirmed open with unchanged completion criteria. If none is available, use a clearly labeled demo mission and remove any reward amount.
posts.repurpose: Turn each step into one Story card and one Discord checklist.

posts.objective: Publish the midpoint field check and choose the next question from real response.
posts.primary_channel: YouTube Live or prerecorded update, Instagram carousel
posts.hook: Fifteen days in, here is what the field lab actually changed.
posts.caption: Report the questions received, qualified visits, first meaningful actions, and one product or content change. Name the measurement window and denominator. If the sample is too small, publish the method and the questions instead of dressing noise as a result.
posts.visual_direction: Four metric cards fed by verified analytics, followed by one screen recording of the change shipped.
posts.cta: Choose the question the final two weeks should investigate.
posts.proof_or_source: First-party analytics and question ledger as of 2026-08-03
posts.guardrail: Hold until every displayed number has a source, window, unit, definition, and denominator, and one claimed change is deployed. Suppress small groups when reporting could expose a participant.
posts.repurpose: Publish the full method on the founder account and the winning question in Discord.
posts.kpi: Qualified votes and week-three activation rate

posts.objective: Make quest review and reward conditions legible.
posts.hook: A reward begins with a visible standard.
posts.caption: Some funded quests offer a cash bounty after an eligible submission passes review. The useful part is the rubric: what counts, who reviews it, and what happens when the evidence needs revision. Show the standard before the reward.
posts.visual_direction: Map the review states visible to a participant in one current funded quest. Blur names and payment details.
posts.cta: Read one quest's completion and eligibility rules before starting.
posts.guardrail: Hold until a funded production quest is open and its participant-visible rules match the post. Do not promise approval, payout timing, or universal cash rewards.
posts.repurpose: Create a reusable quest-rubric template for future launches.
posts.kpi: Eligible submission rate and revision rate

posts.objective: Translate current media research into a useful viewing strategy.
posts.hook: Streaming is a category. Discovery still needs a doorway.
posts.caption: Deloitte's 2025 U.S. survey found 56% of its Gen Z respondents considered social-media content more relevant than television and movies. The cohort definition included minors and adults older than 25. Our test is narrower: short creator-led clips open the question, and a searchable lesson completes it.
posts.visual_direction: Show the finding, cohort definition, sample, and MWA's clip-to-lesson route on one diagram.
posts.cta: Watch the short question, then open the full explanation linked beside it.
posts.guardrail: Do not present the Gen Z result as an ages 18–25 estimate or proof that every young adult prefers short video.
posts.repurpose: Use the full explanation as the source for three concept-first Shorts.
posts.kpi: Short-to-long-form continuation and product click rate

posts.objective: Show Blue reducing choice through implemented guide progress.
posts.hook: A completed guide can turn 'where next?' into three visible options.
posts.caption: This member completed a published guide. Blue showed the next unlocked frontier and the completion that opened it. The learner still chooses the direction; the map makes the reason inspectable.
posts.visual_direction: Use a consenting test participant or the founder account. Show only guide titles and completion state.
posts.cta: Finish one guide and inspect the next unlocked options.
posts.proof_or_source: Recorded guide completion and Blue recommendation; participant consent required
posts.guardrail: Hold until a real completion and resulting recommendation are recorded. Use a founder account in first person or store participant consent. Do not expose field notes, identity, or inferred mental state.
posts.repurpose: Turn the three options into a community vote.

posts.objective: Show multiple entry formats around one educational objective.
posts.hook: Choose your way into one question.
posts.caption: Listen to Blue's overview. Read the canonical guide. Complete the mission. Ask Blue to show the next unlocked route. Each format serves the same question at a different depth.
posts.visual_direction: Four panels using one real topic and four real product surfaces. Link each panel directly.
posts.cta: Choose listen, read, do, or ask, then complete the smallest useful action.
posts.proof_or_source: Blue Radio, published guide map, current mission, and Blue guide-card implementation
posts.guardrail: Hold unless one production topic has a working Blue Radio chapter, published guide, current mission, and Blue guide-card route on publication day. If one route is missing, remove that panel.
posts.repurpose: Create four separate Shorts from the same recording session.

posts.objective: Make the living-library structure understandable.
posts.hook: One guide should leave a trail for the next learner.
posts.caption: Published topics form a prerequisite map. A guide names the ideas underneath it and the questions it can unlock next. Contributions become useful when they improve the canonical path someone else will follow.
posts.visual_direction: Animate one prerequisite path with three nodes. Use only live published titles.
posts.cta: Open one guide and follow a single prerequisite backward.
posts.guardrail: Do not publish hard-coded guide counts or reviewer credentials.
posts.repurpose: Post the three-node path as a saveable static graphic.

posts.objective: Explain what a virtual host can contribute without claiming learning efficacy.
posts.hook: Can a virtual host help you find the next Academy action?
posts.caption: In a peer-reviewed qualitative study, 301 VTuber viewers ages 18–26 described content, personality, avatar design, interaction, community, companionship, and task usefulness as reasons they watched. Participants already watched VTubers, were recruited from Reddit communities, and reported no nationality data. Our question for Blue is practical: can the host help someone find and complete one Academy action?
posts.visual_direction: Put the study limitations on screen beside Blue completing a platform route.
posts.cta: After the demonstration, reply with the next action you would take.
posts.guardrail: Do not claim VTubers improve learning, treat loneliness, or represent all Gen Z viewers.
posts.repurpose: Cut the task demonstration into a Short; keep the full method in the description.
posts.kpi: Correct next-action replies and qualified feature opens

posts.objective: Show how the current Blue Radio episode works and choose one chapter for a longer follow-up.
posts.hook: The first Blue Radio episode runs 4 minutes and 46 seconds.
posts.caption: Ten prerecorded chapters loop through the day and night. Blue's voice level drives her broadcast motion. Each chapter introduces one Academy surface or practice. The current episode is a concise orientation broadcast.
posts.visual_direction: Show the manifest, chapter changes, waveform, and avatar motion. Label the episode prerecorded.
posts.cta: Choose the chapter that should become a full episode.
posts.guardrail: Disclose prerecorded status. Do not describe proposed transcripts or episodes as shipped.
posts.repurpose: Publish one technical clip for builders and one plain-language clip for learners.

posts.objective: Connect one broadcast chapter directly to a completed action.
posts.primary_channel: Blue Radio, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels
posts.hook: Hear the field-notes chapter. Open today's available page.
posts.caption: Blue introduces field notes in the Academy. After the chapter, open today's available entry and start writing. The broadcast gives the map; the writing gives it weight.
posts.visual_direction: Record the chapter title, cut to Field Notes, and complete a three-line demo entry.
posts.cta: Tune in, then write one field note.
posts.guardrail: Do not imply the broadcast observes or remembers what the listener heard.
posts.repurpose: Create one clip per Blue Radio chapter over the next production cycle.

posts.objective: Turn privacy into a concrete product and content standard.
posts.hook: Privacy becomes credible when the behavior is visible.
posts.caption: Use a pseudonymous username and keep field-note text off camera. Before sharing a product demonstration, check every visible username, avatar, account identifier, reflection, and activity record. We will publish broader privacy claims after documenting what each surface collects, exposes, and sends to analytics or advertising systems.
posts.visual_direction: Show an anonymous profile state, a redacted field note, and a plain-language data-use checklist. Confirm each screen in production first.
posts.cta: Open the notice beside one privacy-sensitive action.
posts.proof_or_source: FTC BetterHelp final order (2023); Pew privacy survey (2023); EDITORIAL.md v4.0
posts.guardrail: Hold until a production privacy audit documents notice text, public-profile behavior, field-note visibility, analytics flows, and advertising exclusions. Remove any claim the audit does not support.
posts.repurpose: Add the checklist to creator briefs and demo-recording standards.
posts.kpi: Privacy-notice opens and qualified trust questions

posts.objective: Feature a real mission result with conditions and consent.
posts.hook: The useful part of a reward is the work it can prove.
posts.caption: Show one eligible submission, the rubric it met, the review decision, and the learning artifact it produced. If the quest carried a reward, state the amount, conditions, and date only with the participant's permission and a verified reward record.
posts.visual_direction: Annotate the submission against the rubric. Redact identity and sensitive content according to the participant's preference.
posts.cta: Read the rubric before choosing an open quest.
posts.proof_or_source: Verified production quest, review record, ledger record, and participant consent required
posts.guardrail: Hold if any evidence or permission is missing. No typical-earnings implication. Disclose any creator compensation.
posts.repurpose: Turn the rubric annotations into a reusable 'what strong evidence looks like' guide.

posts.objective: Frame credits as a receipt for verified participation.
posts.hook: The work comes first. The credit records what passed review.
posts.caption: Complete the task and submit the required evidence. A quest creator or authorized staff reviewer checks proof-required work; server rules verify eligible system-recorded tasks. Approved work can earn credits under the quest's published conditions. The standard stays visible from the start.
posts.visual_direction: Use a real low-risk credit flow or a clearly labeled staged demo. Show the rubric and approval state before the reward moment.
posts.cta: Open one quest and inspect what the reviewer will check.
posts.proof_or_source: Current quest-review and credit-award implementation; EDITORIAL.md v4.0
posts.guardrail: Hold until the current review and credit flow is verified in production or a staged demo is labeled on screen. Use 'credits' in outward copy. Make no claim of guaranteed approval, value, or financial appreciation.
posts.repurpose: Create a three-frame explainer for the landing-page FAQ backlog.
posts.kpi: Quest-detail opens and eligible completion rate

posts.objective: Translate online-learning preferences into a small product choice.
posts.format: Evidence card plus three entry paths
posts.hook: Five minutes, a full lesson, or something to hear while you move.
posts.caption: A 2025 survey of 2,032 U.S. adults ages 18–24 who were attending or considering college found flexibility, affordability, and self-paced learning were common reasons to consider online study. The publisher and incomplete methodology limit generalization. The practical test is simple: offer the same objective at three depths and see which path people complete.
posts.visual_direction: Three cards: five-minute mission, full guide, Blue Radio chapter. Put the survey caveat in the footer.
posts.cta: Choose the amount of time you have and complete one action.
posts.guardrail: Call the finding directional. Do not claim young adults broadly prefer online education.
posts.repurpose: Use the three choices as onboarding copy test variants.

posts.objective: Show the ordered 12-week course as a coherent path.
posts.hook: Each week stands on the one before it.
posts.caption: Creative Healing is organized into twelve season-timed weeks. Each week pairs a reading with coursework. Complete every task in the current week to seal it; future weeks can be previewed, then completed when the season reaches them. The schedule gives the work a structure.
posts.visual_direction: Show the current production course surface and one week-sealing flow. Avoid unreleased weeks or private learner content.
posts.cta: Open the current week and complete one task.
posts.guardrail: Hold until the current production course titles, access rules, and week-sealing flow match the recording. Show no unreleased week or private learner content, and promise no outcome beyond the curriculum on screen.
posts.repurpose: Cut one clip for each course stage for the next month.

posts.objective: Close the user-learning loop with a real product or copy change.
posts.hook: Someone got stuck here. This is what changed.
posts.caption: Show the exact point of confusion, the evidence that it recurred, and the smallest change shipped. Then demonstrate the revised route. The person who raised it can remain anonymous, receive credit, or appear directly—their choice.
posts.visual_direction: Before-and-after screen recording with one highlighted friction point and one changed outcome.
posts.cta: Complete the revised route.
posts.proof_or_source: Observed session or repeated support question, shipped change, and consent/attribution record required
posts.guardrail: Hold until the recurring friction evidence, deployed change, and consent or attribution preference are documented. Do not expose support content or invent user feedback.
posts.repurpose: Add the change to the monthly release note and community follow-up.

posts.objective: Publish the month's evidence and open the next learning loop.
posts.hook: Thirty days of fieldwork. Here is what the Academy learned.
posts.caption: Report the questions investigated, lessons published, meaningful actions completed, returning learners, guide revisions, and product changes. Name every metric, window, denominator, and limitation. Close with one next experiment grounded in what people actually did.
posts.visual_direction: A calm field-report deck: question, method, evidence, limitation, change, next experiment. Use current verified product captures.
posts.cta: Open one current mission and read its completion criteria.
posts.proof_or_source: First-party analytics, source ledger, change log, and consent records through 2026-08-18
posts.guardrail: Hold until every total has a source, window, definition, denominator, and limitation, and every participant artifact has permission. Publish no scarcity claim, resetting countdown, broad outcome claim, or press pitch without verifiable news.
posts.repurpose: Turn the report into a landing-page proof backlog and a press note only if the evidence is genuinely newsworthy.
posts.kpi: First meaningful actions and seven-day return from the closing report
